mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces the opportunity cost of holding risk assets, producing a structural positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during FEDFUNDS<0.25% periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p>0.05), or rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which forward-looking equity markets price in as improved corporate earnings prospects over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by >= 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive 20-day forward return frequency below 55% or no statistically significant difference from unconditional base rate of 65.5% positive days.

mechanism_narrative: Joint occurrence of accelerating nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains anchored near zero indicates a risk-on labor-driven expansion without imminent monetary tightening, jointly producing above-average 20-day forward equity returns that neither series alone would predict.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND UNRATE declining AND FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion under accommodative policy
failure_condition: Joint-trigger subsample fails to outperform single-factor (PAYEMS-only or UNRATE-only) signals by a statistically significant margin in walk-forward testing, or Rank IC of interaction term is not incremental over additive baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending, but this effect on forward equity returns is amplified specifically when the policy rate is pinned near zero, because low financing costs extend the durability of consumption-led growth; outside a zero-rate regime the same RSAFS strength has muted predictive power due to competing rate-driven valuation pressure.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary regime with consumer-led demand growth
failure_condition: Predictive strength (Rank IC) of RSAFS growth on 60-day forward returns does not differ significantly between FEDFUNDS<0.25% and FEDFUNDS>1% subsamples, disproving the regime dependency.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which is continuously priced into near-term equity returns as a leading indicator of corporate revenue momentum over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO month-over-month change has Rank IC below 0.02 across rolling 1-year windows for more than half of the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical threshold signals deteriorating residential investment and consumer confidence, historically a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as markets reprice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=893.0 thousand units as of 2014-06-01 vintage
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST decline episodes show 60-day forward SPX returns positive in more than 55% of instances, or no significant negative mean return versus unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPI inflation above trend raises market expectations of earlier Federal Reserve tightening, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once CPI year-over-year growth crosses above 2.5%.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=237.231 revised value as of 2014-06-01 vintage
regime_hint: rising inflation ahead of monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following CPI>2.5% YoY triggers are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) or positive-return frequency exceeds 55% in triggered subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) alongside quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) captures broad-based mid-cycle economic momentum that individually each series understates; this combined momentum signal predicts positive 60-day forward S&P 500 returns specifically during mid-cycle expansion phases, but not during late-cycle or recessionary regimes where the same combination reflects overheating risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS 3-month growth > 0.4% AND GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2%
failure_condition: During mid-cycle-labeled periods, joint-trigger instances fail to outperform the unconditional 65.5% positive-day base rate, or the interaction signal's incremental Rank IC over single-series baselines is not statistically distinguishable from zero.
